exercises.title: LEAST COMMON MULTIPLE
exercises.brief: Smallest number divisible by both inputs.
promptLines: Define **lcm(a, b)** returning the least common multiple of two positive integers. Use the identity `a * b / gcd(a, b)`.
starterLines: // TODO: divide the product by the gcd
solutionLines: function gcd(x, y) { return y === 0 ? x : gcd(y, x % y); }
exercises.hint: Compute gcd first, then a / gcd(a,b) * b avoids overflow.
exercises.lore: Two clock cycles realign only at their shared beat.

exercises.title: DIGITAL ROOT
exercises.brief: Collapse a number to a single digit.
promptLines: Define **digitalRoot(n)** that repeatedly sums the digits of `n` (n >= 0) until a single digit remains.
starterLines: // TODO: loop until n is a single digit
exercises.hint: Outer loop while n>=10; inner loop sums digits.

exercises.title: POWER OF TWO
promptLines: Define **isPowerOfTwo(n)** returning `true` only when `n` is a positive power of two (1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ...).
starterLines: // TODO: a power of two has exactly one set bit
exercises.hint: n > 0 AND (n & (n - 1)) === 0 clears the lone set bit.
exercises.lore: Powers of two: a single lit bit in the dark register.

exercises.title: ROUND TO PLACES
exercises.brief: Round to a fixed number of decimals.
promptLines: Define **roundTo(n, places)** that rounds `n` to `places` decimal places and returns a Number. Scale up, round, scale back down.
starterLines: // TODO: scale by 10**places, round, then unscale
exercises.lore: Trim the signal's noise to the decimals that matter.

exercises.title: SUM OF DIVISORS
exercises.brief: Add up every divisor of n.
promptLines: Define **sumDivisors(n)** returning the sum of all positive divisors of `n` (n >= 1), including 1 and n itself.
starterLines: // TODO: add every i from 1..n that divides n
exercises.hint: Loop i from 1 to n; when n % i === 0, add i to the total.
exercises.lore: Tally a number's factors; if they sum to itself, it is perfect.
